title: Spec: Initial Scaffolding for OpenCode Plugin
description:
# Initial Scaffolding Specification

## Overview

Create the foundational project structure for `open-mardi-gras`, a TypeScript-based OpenCode plugin that provides workflow automation features (starting with command chaining/return functionality). This scaffolding establishes the npm package structure, build system, TypeScript configuration, linting, and open source files required for publishing to the npm registry.

**IMPORTANT**: The `.opencode/` directory contains OpenCode's configuration and helper plugins for development purposes ONLY. It is NOT part of this plugin package and should be completely ignored in the published npm package.

**Goal**: Produce a working npm package that exports OpenCode plugins and can be installed via `npm install open-mardi-gras` or referenced in `opencode.json`.

## Requirements

### 1. Package Configuration

**1.1 Root package.json**
- Package name: `open-mardi-gras`
- Initial version: `0.1.0`
- Description: "OpenCode plugin for workflow automation and command chaining"
- Type: `module` (ES modules)
- Main entry: `./dist/index.js`
- Types entry: `./dist/index.d.ts`
- Files to include: `["dist/", "LICENSE", "README.md", "CHANGELOG.md"]` (explicitly excludes `.opencode/`)
- Scripts needed:
  - `build`: Compile TypeScript to `dist/` (`tsc`)
  - `lint`: Run ESLint (`eslint src/`)
  - `lint:fix`: Fix ESLint issues (`eslint src/ --fix`)
  - `clean`: Remove `dist/` directory (`rm -rf dist/`)
  - `prepublishOnly`: Run `clean` and `build` (clean must run first to avoid stale files)
  - `dev` (optional): Watch mode (`tsc --watch`)
- Engines field:
  - `node`: ">=18.0.0" (supports current LTS)

**1.2 Dependencies**
- `@opencode-ai/plugin`: `^1.2.10` (devDependency only - OpenCode guarantees it's available at runtime)
- `typescript`: `^5.x` (devDependency)
- `@types/node`: `^20.x` (devDependency)
- `eslint`: `^9.x` (devDependency)
- `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin`: `^8.x` (devDependency)
- `@typescript-eslint/parser`: `^8.x` (devDependency)

### 2. TypeScript Configuration

**2.1 tsconfig.json**
- Target: ES2022
- Module: NodeNext
- ModuleResolution: NodeNext
- Declaration: true (generate .d.ts files)
- DeclarationMap: true
- SourceMap: true
- Strict: true
- OutDir: `./dist`
- RootDir: `./src`
- SkipLibCheck: true
- EsModuleInterop: true
- Exclude: `[".opencode/"]` (don't compile dev helpers)

### 3. ESLint Configuration

**3.1 eslint.config.js**
- Use ESLint flat config format (eslint.config.js)
- Include `@typescript-eslint/recommended` rules
- Include `@typescript-eslint/recommended-type-checked` rules
- Target files: `src/**/*.ts`
- Ignore: `dist/`, `node_modules/`, `.opencode/`
- Include parserOptions with project reference to tsconfig.json for type-aware linting

### 4. Project Structure

```
open-mardi-gras/
├── package.json           # NPM package configuration
├── tsconfig.json          # TypeScript compiler configuration
├── eslint.config.js       # ESLint configuration
├── LICENSE                # MIT license file
├── CHANGELOG.md           # Changelog following common-changelog format
├── README.md              # Existing - enhance with development info
├── .gitignore             # Add dist/, node_modules/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts           # Main entry point - exports HelloWorldPlugin
│   └── plugins/
│       └── hello-world.ts # Validation plugin (hello world)
└── dist/                  # Compiled output (gitignored)
```

**Note**: `.opencode/` directory exists at root but is NOT part of the plugin package.

### 5. Source Code Files

**5.1 src/plugins/hello-world.ts**
- Simple "Hello World" OpenCode plugin to validate wiring
- Implements the Plugin type from `@opencode-ai/plugin`
- Logs a message on initialization
- Accepts optional configuration object (even if unused) to support future configuration needs
- Example structure:
  ```typescript
  import type { Plugin } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"
  
  export interface HelloWorldPluginConfig {
    // Reserved for future configuration options
  }
  
  export const HelloWorldPlugin = (config?: HelloWorldPluginConfig): Plugin => {
    return async ({ client }) => {
      await client.app.log({
        body: {
          service: "open-mardi-gras",
          level: "info",
          message: "HelloWorldPlugin initialized",
        },
      })
      
      return {
        // Empty hooks object - this plugin just validates wiring
      }
    }
  }
  ```

**5.2 src/index.ts**
- Main entry point for the plugin package
- Export HelloWorldPlugin from `./plugins/hello-world.js`
- Export HelloWorldPluginConfig type for TypeScript users
- Re-export types from `@opencode-ai/plugin` if needed
- Structure:
  ```typescript
  export { HelloWorldPlugin } from './plugins/hello-world.js'
  export type { HelloWorldPluginConfig } from './plugins/hello-world.js'
  export type * from '@opencode-ai/plugin'
  ```

### 6. Open Source Files

**6.1 LICENSE**
- MIT License
- Include copyright notice: "Copyright (c) 2026 Brandon Dennis"
- Use standard MIT license text

**6.2 CHANGELOG.md**
- Follow [Common Changelog](https://common-changelog.org/) format
- Initial version: 0.1.0
- Sections: "0.1.0 - 2026-02-26" with "Initial release" description
- Include link to full git history for details

**6.3 README.md (Enhancements)**
Add sections:
- Installation (npm install)
- Development setup (bun install, build, lint)
- Plugin usage in opencode.json (both string reference and import with config)
  ```typescript
  // Option 1: String reference (simplest)
  plugins: ['open-mardi-gras']
  
  // Option 2: Import with configuration (for advanced use)
  import { HelloWorldPlugin } from 'open-mardi-gras'
  plugins: [HelloWorldPlugin({ /* options */ })]
  ```
- Contributing (brief)
- License reference
- Changelog reference

### 7. Build System

**7.1 Compilation**
- Use `tsc` for building (no bundler needed for now)
- Output to `dist/` directory
- Include .d.ts files for TypeScript consumers
- Include source maps for debugging
- Must NOT compile `.opencode/` directory

**7.2 Watch Mode**
- `dev` script using `tsc --watch`

### 8. Git Configuration

**8.1 .gitignore additions**
```
# Build output
dist/

# Dependencies
node_modules/

# OS
.DS_Store

# Temporary files
*.tmp
*.swp
```

**8.2 Do NOT add to .gitignore**
- `.opencode/` - this directory IS tracked in git (contains dev helpers)

**8.3 npm publish exclusions**
- Use `files` field in package.json: `["dist/", "LICENSE", "README.md", "CHANGELOG.md"]`
- This explicitly excludes `.opencode/` from npm package

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Running `bun install` installs all dependencies
- [ ] Running `bun run build` compiles TypeScript without errors
- [ ] Running `bun run lint` checks code style without critical errors
- [ ] Running `bun run lint:fix` fixes auto-fixable ESLint issues
- [ ] Running `bun run clean` removes the `dist/` directory
- [ ] Running `bun run dev` starts TypeScript watch mode
- [ ] `dist/` directory contains compiled `.js` and `.d.ts` files
- [ ] `dist/` contains `.js.map` source map files
- [ ] `dist/` contains `.d.ts.map` declaration map files
- [ ] `dist/index.js` is the main entry point
- [ ] `dist/index.d.ts` provides TypeScript definitions
- [ ] `tsconfig.json` is valid (running `tsc --noEmit` succeeds)
- [ ] `eslint.config.js` is valid (running `eslint --print-config src/index.ts` succeeds)
- [ ] LICENSE file exists with standard MIT text and copyright "Brandon Dennis"
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md exists following common-changelog format with date 2026-02-26
- [ ] README includes development instructions
- [ ] README documents both string and import plugin usage patterns
- [ ] Package can be linked locally with `bun link` for testing
- [ ] Running `npm pack` creates a valid tarball without errors
- [ ] Running `npm pack --dry-run` shows only expected files (dist/, LICENSE, README.md, CHANGELOG.md)
- [ ] Running `npm publish --dry-run` succeeds without errors
- [ ] `files` field in package.json correctly limits published files
- [ ] `.opencode/` directory is NOT included in the published npm package
- [ ] `.opencode/` directory IS tracked in git (not gitignored)
- [ ] `git check-ignore dist/` returns dist/ (is ignored)
- [ ] `git check-ignore node_modules/` returns node_modules/ (is ignored)
- [ ] @opencode-ai/plugin is NOT in dependencies or peerDependencies (devDependency only)
- [ ] HelloWorldPlugin can be imported and used in an OpenCode configuration
- [ ] Installing the package via `npm install` and importing it works without errors
- [ ] Plugin accepts optional configuration object (even if unused in v0.1.0)

## Edge Cases

### Bun vs npm Compatibility
- Use `bun` for development (as per OpenCode)
- Ensure package works with standard `npm`/`node` consumers
- Lock file: Use `bun.lock` (Bun's format)
- Windows compatibility: `clean` script uses `rm -rf` (Unix-only); Windows support deferred to future release

### ESM-Only Package
- Do not generate CommonJS output
- Set `"type": "module"` in package.json
- Use `.js` extensions in imports (TypeScript handles this)
- Ensure `"module": "NodeNext"` in tsconfig.json

### Dependency Handling
- `@opencode-ai/plugin` is a devDependency only
- OpenCode guarantees runtime availability
- No peerDependencies complexity needed
- Type checking works during development

### Build Artifacts
- `dist/` is gitignored but included in npm package via `files` field
- Source maps included for debugging
- Declaration maps for IDE go-to-definition

### .opencode/ Directory Handling
- **In Git**: `.opencode/` IS tracked (helpers shared across dev environments)
- **In Build**: TypeScript excludes `.opencode/` from compilation
- **In npm**: `.opencode/` is NOT published (via `files` field in package.json)
- **In ESLint**: Ignored from linting (development helpers, not package code)

### Build Failures
- If TypeScript compilation fails, output must show file/line/column of errors
- Build process should exit with non-zero status code on failure
- `clean` script must complete successfully before `build` runs (order matters in prepublishOnly)

### Stale Build Artifacts
- `clean` script must remove entire `dist/` directory before rebuilding
- Stale files in dist/ must not be included in published package

### npm Package Verification
- `npm pack --dry-run` must show only expected files
- Published package must not include .opencode/, src/, or config files
- Package must be installable via `npm install` without errors

### Runtime Errors
- Plugin must fail gracefully if OpenCode client is unavailable
- Missing @opencode-ai/plugin at runtime should produce clear error message

## Dependencies

This epic has no dependencies. It is the foundational work.

## Child Issues

After this scaffolding is complete, the following child issues will be created:

1. **"Implement Return Plugin"** (separate epic)
   - Depends on: This scaffolding epic
   - Implements the command chaining feature described in README
   - Adds return.ts to src/plugins/
   - Updates index.ts exports

## Next Steps After Scaffolding

1. Verify scaffolding acceptance criteria all pass
2. Close this epic
3. Create child epic: "Implement Return Plugin" (depends on this epic)
4. Test locally with `bun link`
5. Create npm account and publish v0.1.0
6. Update documentation with real npm install instructions

## References

- OpenCode Plugin Documentation: https://opencode.ai/docs/plugins/
- Bun Documentation: https://bun.sh/docs
- TypeScript ESM Guide: https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/esm-node.html
- npm Package Publishing: https://docs.npmjs.com/packages-and-modules/contributing-packages-to-the-registry
- npm files field: https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v10/configuring-npm/package-json#files
- Common Changelog: https://common-changelog.org/

title: Create ESLint configuration (eslint.config.js)
description:
## Task: Create ESLint configuration (eslint.config.js)

**Location:** `eslint.config.js`

**Requirements:**
- Use ESLint flat config format (eslint.config.js)
- Include `@typescript-eslint/recommended` rules
- Include `@typescript-eslint/recommended-type-checked` rules
- Target files: `src/**/*.ts`
- Ignore: `dist/`, `node_modules/`, `.opencode/`
- Include parserOptions with project reference to tsconfig.json for type-aware linting

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] eslint.config.js is valid JavaScript
- [ ] Running `eslint --print-config src/index.ts` succeeds
- [ ] Running `bun run lint` checks code style without critical errors
- [ ] Running `bun run lint:fix` fixes auto-fixable ESLint issues
- [ ] `.opencode/` directory is ignored from linting

**Edge Cases:**
- Type-aware linting requires parserOptions.project pointing to tsconfig.json
- Flat config format (not .eslintrc.json)

title: Create directory structure and source files
description:
## Task: Create directory structure and source files

**Locations:** 
- `src/index.ts`
- `src/plugins/hello-world.ts`

**Directory Structure to Create:**
```
src/
├── index.ts           # Main entry point - exports HelloWorldPlugin
└── plugins/
    └── hello-world.ts # Validation plugin
```

**src/plugins/hello-world.ts Requirements:**
- Simple "Hello World" OpenCode plugin to validate wiring
- Implements the Plugin type from `@opencode-ai/plugin`
- Logs a message on initialization using client.app.log()
- Accepts optional configuration object (HelloWorldPluginConfig interface)
- Returns empty hooks object

Code structure:
```typescript
import type { Plugin } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"

export interface HelloWorldPluginConfig {
  // Reserved for future configuration options
}

export const HelloWorldPlugin = (config?: HelloWorldPluginConfig): Plugin => {
  return async ({ client }) => {
    await client.app.log({
      body: {
        service: "open-mardi-gras",
        level: "info",
        message: "HelloWorldPlugin initialized",
      },
    })
    
    return {
      // Empty hooks object - this plugin just validates wiring
    }
  }
}
```

**src/index.ts Requirements:**
- Main entry point for the plugin package
- Export HelloWorldPlugin from `./plugins/hello-world.js`
- Export HelloWorldPluginConfig type for TypeScript users
- Re-export types from `@opencode-ai/plugin`
- Use `.js` extensions in imports (TypeScript handles this)

Code structure:
```typescript
export { HelloWorldPlugin } from './plugins/hello-world.js'
export type { HelloWorldPluginConfig } from './plugins/hello-world.js'
export type * from '@opencode-ai/plugin'
```

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Directory structure exists: `src/` and `src/plugins/`
- [ ] hello-world.ts implements Plugin type correctly
- [ ] index.ts exports HelloWorldPlugin and HelloWorldPluginConfig
- [ ] Code follows TypeScript strict mode
- [ ] Running `bun run build` compiles without errors
- [ ] `dist/` contains compiled `.js` and `.d.ts` files
- [ ] `dist/index.js` is the main entry point
- [ ] `dist/index.d.ts` provides TypeScript definitions

**Edge Cases:**
- ESM imports must use `.js` extensions (TypeScript rewrites these)
- Plugin accepts optional config object (even if unused in v0.1.0)

title: Create LICENSE file (MIT)
description:
## Task: Create LICENSE file (MIT)

**Location:** `LICENSE`

**Requirements:**
- MIT License
- Copyright notice: "Copyright (c) 2026 Brandon Dennis"
- Standard MIT license text

MIT License template:
```
MIT License

Copyright (c) 2026 Brandon Dennis

Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, to any person obtaining a copy
of this software and associated documentation files (the "Software"), to deal
in the Software without restriction, including without limitation the rights
to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell
copies of the Software, and to permit persons to whom the Software is
furnished to do so, subject to the following conditions:

The above copyright notice and this permission notice shall be included in all
copies or substantial portions of the Software.

THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR
IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY,
FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONINFRINGEMENT. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE
AUTHORS OR COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER
LIABILITY, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM,
OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN THE
SOFTWARE.
```

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] LICENSE file exists at repository root
- [ ] Contains standard MIT license text
- [ ] Copyright year is 2026
- [ ] Copyright holder is "Brandon Dennis"
- [ ] Running `npm pack --dry-run` includes LICENSE file

**Edge Cases:**
- Ensure file is not gitignored
- File must be included in npm package via files field in package.json

title: Create CHANGELOG.md following common-changelog format
description:
## Task: Create CHANGELOG.md following common-changelog format

**Location:** `CHANGELOG.md`

**Requirements:**
- Follow [Common Changelog](https://common-changelog.org/) format
- Initial version: 0.1.0
- Date: 2026-02-26
- Sections: "0.1.0 - 2026-02-26" with "Initial release" description
- Include link to full git history for details

Common Changelog Format Example:
```markdown
# Changelog

## 0.1.0 - 2026-02-26

Initial release of open-mardi-gras.

### Added
- Initial scaffolding with TypeScript build system
- HelloWorldPlugin for validation
- ESLint configuration with TypeScript support
- NPM package configuration for ESM-only distribution

See the full git history for detailed changes.
```

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md exists at repository root
- [ ] Follows common-changelog format
- [ ] Version 0.1.0 with date 2026-02-26
- [ ] Running `npm pack --dry-run` includes CHANGELOG.md

**Edge Cases:**
- Ensure file is not gitignored
- File must be included in npm package via files field in package.json

title: Update README.md with development documentation
description:
## Task: Update README.md with development documentation

**Location:** `README.md` (enhance existing file)

**Requirements:**
Add the following sections to the existing README:

1. **Installation**
   - npm install open-mardi-gras

2. **Development Setup**
   - bun install
   - bun run build
   - bun run lint
   - bun run dev (watch mode)

3. **Plugin Usage in opencode.json**
   Show both patterns:
   ```typescript
   // Option 1: String reference (simplest)
   plugins: ['open-mardi-gras']
   
   // Option 2: Import with configuration
   import { HelloWorldPlugin } from 'open-mardi-gras'
   plugins: [HelloWorldPlugin({ /* options */ })]
   ```

4. **Contributing** (brief section)

5. **License** (reference MIT license)

6. **Changelog** (reference CHANGELOG.md)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] README.md includes installation instructions
- [ ] README.md includes development setup instructions
- [ ] README.md documents both string and import plugin usage patterns
- [ ] Running `npm pack --dry-run` includes README.md
- [ ] Documentation is clear and complete

**Edge Cases:**
- Preserve any existing content
- Ensure file is not gitignored
- File must be included in npm package via files field in package.json

title: Configure .gitignore for build artifacts
description:
## Task: Configure .gitignore for build artifacts

**Location:** `.gitignore` (update existing file)

**Requirements:**
Add the following entries to .gitignore:

```
# Build output
dist/

# Dependencies
node_modules/

# OS
.DS_Store

# Temporary files
*.tmp
*.swp
```

**Important:** Do NOT add `.opencode/` to .gitignore
- The `.opencode/` directory IS tracked in git (contains dev helpers)
- It is excluded from npm package via package.json files field
- It is excluded from TypeScript compilation via tsconfig.json exclude

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] .gitignore exists at repository root
- [ ] `dist/` is gitignored
- [ ] `node_modules/` is gitignored
- [ ] OS files (.DS_Store) are gitignored
- [ ] Temporary files (*.tmp, *.swp) are gitignored
- [ ] Running `git check-ignore dist/` returns dist/ (is ignored)
- [ ] Running `git check-ignore node_modules/` returns node_modules/ (is ignored)
- [ ] `.opencode/` is NOT gitignored (remains tracked)

**Edge Cases:**
- Ensure `.opencode/` stays tracked in git
- dist/ is gitignored but included in npm via files field

title: Build and verify the package
description:
## Task: Build and verify the package

**Requirements:**
Run the full build and verification process:

1. **Build Process:**
   - Run `bun run clean` to remove stale dist/
   - Run `bun run build` to compile TypeScript
   - Verify dist/ contains:
     - Compiled `.js` files
     - `.d.ts` declaration files
     - `.js.map` source maps
     - `.d.ts.map` declaration maps

2. **Lint Verification:**
   - Run `bun run lint` - should pass without critical errors

3. **NPM Package Verification:**
   - Run `npm pack --dry-run` and verify only expected files shown:
     - dist/
     - LICENSE
     - README.md
     - CHANGELOG.md
   - Run `npm publish --dry-run` - should succeed
   - Verify `.opencode/` is NOT in the package

4. **Link Testing:**
   - Run `bun link` for local testing
   - Verify package can be imported

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `bun run build` compiles TypeScript without errors
- [ ] `bun run lint` passes without critical errors
- [ ] `dist/` contains `.js`, `.d.ts`, `.js.map`, `.d.ts.map` files
- [ ] `dist/index.js` is the main entry point
- [ ] `dist/index.d.ts` provides TypeScript definitions
- [ ] `npm pack --dry-run` shows only expected files
- [ ] `npm publish --dry-run` succeeds without errors
- [ ] `.opencode/` directory is NOT included in npm package
- [ ] `bun link` works for local testing
- [ ] HelloWorldPlugin can be imported and used

**Edge Cases:**
- Clean must complete before build in prepublishOnly
- Verify no stale files in dist/
- Package must be installable via npm install

title: Code review: Initial Scaffolding for OpenCode Plugin
description:
## Task: Code review - Initial Scaffolding for OpenCode Plugin

**Description:**
Invoke the reviewer agent (@bd-reviewer) to perform a thorough code review of all changes in this epic. The reviewer will file beads for every finding using discovered-from links. Close this bead only when the review is complete.

**Scope:**
Review all files created in this epic:
- package.json
- tsconfig.json
- eslint.config.js
- src/index.ts
- src/plugins/hello-world.ts
- LICENSE
- CHANGELOG.md
- README.md
- .gitignore

**Review Checklist:**
- [ ] Package configuration correct (ESM, files field, scripts)
- [ ] TypeScript configuration valid and strict
- [ ] ESLint configuration follows flat config format
- [ ] Source files compile without errors
- [ ] Plugin implements correct types from @opencode-ai/plugin
- [ ] LICENSE has correct copyright
- [ ] CHANGELOG follows common-changelog format
- [ ] README documents all required sections
- [ ] .gitignore correctly excludes build artifacts
- [ ] Build and verify tasks pass all acceptance criteria
- [ ] No .opencode/ files in npm package
- [ ] All acceptance criteria from spec are met

**Process:**
1. Run `@bd-reviewer` agent on all changes
2. Address any findings filed as discovered-from beads
3. Verify all acceptance criteria pass
4. Close this bead when review is complete

- It is excluded from TypeScript compilation via tsconfig.json exclude

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] .gitignore exists at repository root
- [ ]  is gitignored
- [ ]  is gitignored
- [ ] OS files (.DS_Store) are gitignored
- [ ] Temporary files (*.tmp, *.swp) are gitignored
- [ ] Running  returns dist/ (is ignored)
- [ ] Running  returns node_modules/ (is ignored)
- [ ]  is NOT gitignored (remains tracked)

**Edge Cases:**
- Ensure  stays tracked in git
- dist/ is gitignored but included in npm via files field

title: Create package.json with NPM configuration
description:
## Task: Create package.json with NPM configuration

**Location:** `package.json`

**Requirements:**
- Package name: `open-mardi-gras`
- Initial version: `0.1.0`
- Description: "OpenCode plugin for workflow automation and command chaining"
- Type: `module` (ES modules)
- Main entry: `./dist/index.js`
- Types entry: `./dist/index.d.ts`
- Files to include: `["dist/", "LICENSE", "README.md", "CHANGELOG.md"]` (explicitly excludes `.opencode/`)
- Scripts needed:
  - `build`: Compile TypeScript to `dist/` (`tsc`)
  - `lint`: Run ESLint (`eslint src/`)
  - `lint:fix`: Fix ESLint issues (`eslint src/ --fix`)
  - `clean`: Remove `dist/` directory (`rm -rf dist/`)
  - `prepublishOnly`: Run `clean` and `build` (clean must run first to avoid stale files)
  - `dev` (optional): Watch mode (`tsc --watch`)
- Engines field:
  - `node`: ">=18.0.0"

**Dependencies (devDependencies only):**
- `@opencode-ai/plugin`: `^1.2.10`
- `typescript`: `^5.x`
- `@types/node`: `^20.x`
- `eslint`: `^9.x`
- `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin`: `^8.x`
- `@typescript-eslint/parser`: `^8.x`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] package.json is valid JSON
- [ ] All required fields present
- [ ] @opencode-ai/plugin is in devDependencies only (not dependencies or peerDependencies)
- [ ] files field limits published files correctly
- [ ] Running `bun install` installs all dependencies without errors

**Edge Cases:**
- Ensure `"type": "module"` for ESM-only package
- No CommonJS output
- Windows compatibility: clean script uses rm -rf (Unix-only); defer Windows support to future release

title: Create TypeScript configuration (tsconfig.json)
description:
## Task: Create TypeScript configuration (tsconfig.json)

**Location:** `tsconfig.json`

**Requirements:**
- Target: ES2022
- Module: NodeNext
- ModuleResolution: NodeNext
- Declaration: true (generate .d.ts files)
- DeclarationMap: true
- SourceMap: true
- Strict: true
- OutDir: `./dist`
- RootDir: `./src`
- SkipLibCheck: true
- EsModuleInterop: true
- Exclude: `[".opencode/"]` (don't compile dev helpers)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] tsconfig.json is valid JSON
- [ ] Running `tsc --noEmit` succeeds without errors
- [ ] TypeScript can compile to `dist/` directory
- [ ] `.opencode/` directory is excluded from compilation
- [ ] ESM configuration correct (NodeNext module system)

**Edge Cases:**
- Ensure module resolution works with ESM imports
- Source maps enabled for debugging
- Declaration maps for IDE go-to-definition
